title: Crepidaria padifolia
taxonomy: Crepidaria padifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It belongs to the order Lamiales and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant's classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the clade Angiosperms. As a member of the Gesneriaceae family, Crepidaria padifolia shares characteristics with other species of flowering plants that are adapted to tropical environments. Its classification is a way to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and adaptations to its native habitat.

The plant's extracts are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating wounds and skin conditions.
